title: Raw Russian Vegetables Salad
description: A spicy, aerated, monsoon-inspired dish featuring raw vegetables, katsuramuki-cut, jain-friendly. Perfect for a light and refreshing meal during the summer months.
instructions: Peel the carrots and beets and grate them using a box grater.
Thinly slice the cucumbers and separate them into individual pieces.
In a large bowl, combine the grated carrots and beets, sliced cucumbers, thinly sliced onions, and minced garlic.
In a small bowl, whisk together the freshly squeezed lime juice and olive oil to make the dressing.
Add the chopped fresh dill and spicy harissa sauce to the dressing and whisk until well combined.
Pour the dressing over the vegetables and toss to coat.
Season the salad with salt and pepper to taste.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, use a vegetable peeler to cut the katsuramuki-style pieces from the carrots and beets, and add them to the salad.
Toss the salad gently to combine and serve chilled.
